channel.title: Toby Qian — Journal
channel.description: Essays from desk, screen and street.

item.title: On building small tools
item.description: Why one-page utilities still beat the fanciest framework — and what they teach you about taste.

item.title: How a student put himself on the internet
item.description: Building this site from a blank file — the choices, the lessons, the small joys of vanilla CSS.

item.title: Games worth bragging about
item.description: Not a review — honest favorites and the moments that made them stick.

item.title: "No man steps in the same river twice."
item.description: Three years downstream from an exam that was meant to decide everything — on time, the open question, and the mercy of being able to look back.

item.title: "Love consists in this…"
item.description: A late-evening note on the kind of love that lets two solitudes stay separate — and on attention as the form love takes when one chooses to give it.

item.title: "Anxiety is the dizziness of freedom."
item.description: On the season of comparing oneself, the weight of an undecided future, and the slow work of taking the stone back into one's own hands.

item.title: "Il faut imaginer Sisyphe heureux."
item.description: One must imagine Sisyphus happy. A small answer to the largest question I keep asking — that the meaning is not at the end, it is in the experiencing: every field not yet seen, every thing not yet attempted, every hour not yet lived.
